prompts.text: {player1}, press START! Take 2 sips to begin.
{player1}, player one has entered the game! Give out 3 sips to the lobby.
{player1}, it's dangerous to go alone — take this sip!
{player1}, loading screen... take 2 sips while we buffer.
{player1}, achievement unlocked: First Sip. Take 2.
{player1}, the tutorial is over. Give out 2 sips — the real game starts now.
First to shout it out: What accessory was bundled with Donkey Kong 64 for the N64? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Expansion Pak — DK64 was the first game to require it

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Before making video games, what product did Nintendo sell in the 1800s? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hanafuda playing cards (founded 1889)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the Nintendo GameCube's development codename? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Dolphin — which is why the emulator is called Dolphin

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many buttons does a standard N64 controller have? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 14 buttons (including the D-pad directions, A, B, C-buttons, Z, L, R, Start)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the original retail price of the Nintendo 64 at US launch in 1996? Winner gives out 3 sips.
First to shout it out: What color was the debug/development Nintendo 64 unit? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Green — developers used green N64 dev kits

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the Wii's development codename? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Revolution

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the Nintendo Switch's codename before reveal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: NX

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What portable console had a 'Virtual Boy' predecessor that displayed red and black graphics? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Virtual Boy itself — Nintendo's 1995 'portable' 3D console that flopped

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What year did the original Game Boy launch in Japan? Winner gives out 3 sips.
First to shout it out: In Pokémon Red/Blue, what happens when you use Strength on the truck near the S.S. Anne? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Nothing — it was a famous playground rumor that Mew was under the truck, but it's just decoration

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Pokémon was originally supposed to be the mascot instead of Pikachu? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Clefairy — Pikachu was chosen because of the anime's popularity

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Pokémon Gold/Silver, what real-world event inspired the plot about the Radio Tower takeover? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It was inspired by Team Rocket's disbandment 3 years prior in Gen 1 — no specific real event, but the Radio Tower is based on Osaka's Tsutenkaku Tower

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What glitch in Pokémon Red/Blue lets you encounter MissingNo? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Old Man glitch — talk to the old man in Viridian City, then fly to Cinnabar Island and surf along the east coast

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is Pikachu's Pokédex number? Winner gives out 2 sips.
First to shout it out: How many original Pokémon were in Gen 1? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 151 (including Mew)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real animal is Poliwag's swirl pattern based on? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Transparent tadpoles — you can see their intestines spiraling, which inspired the belly swirl

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was Pokémon called in early development before 'Pocket Monsters'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Capsule Monsters (Capumon)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what game can you find a hidden message from the developers by checking the back of a truck at the harbor? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Trick question — there's no message under the truck in Pokémon Red/Blue. It's gaming's most famous myth.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What two Pokémon games were the first to feature a real-time clock? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Pokémon Gold and Silver

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was Mario's original name in Donkey Kong (1981)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Jumpman

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Mario was named after the landlord of Nintendo of America's warehouse. What was his last name? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mario Segale — full name Mario Segale

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Super Mario Bros., the bushes and clouds use the same sprite — just different colors. True or false? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: True — the cloud sprite and bush sprite are identical, just palette-swapped

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What job did Mario have before being a plumber? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Carpenter (in the original Donkey Kong)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many worlds are in the original Super Mario Bros.? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 8 worlds (8-4 is the final level)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does the Super Star (invincibility star) music in Mario speed up? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It actually plays a sped-up version of the level's main theme — the tempo increases

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Super Mario 64, what is the minimum number of stars needed to beat the game? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 70 stars (or 0 with the BLJ glitch in speedruns)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the name of the Chain Chomp enemy that Mario can set free in Super Mario 64? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Chain Chomp in Bob-omb Battlefield — ground pound its stake 3 times to free it and get a star

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game marked Luigi's first appearance? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Mario Bros. (1983 arcade) — NOT Super Mario Bros.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Mario Kart 64, what is the famous shortcut in Rainbow Road? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: At the start, you can jump off the ramp and land on a lower section of track, skipping about 2/3 of the first lap

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Ocarina of Time, what happens if you wait in the Temple of Time for 7 in-game years? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Nothing — you have to pull the Master Sword. The 7-year skip is triggered by the sword, not actual time passing

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the first Zelda game to feature fishing? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Link's Awakening (1993, Game Boy) — the fishing mini-game was a first

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Breath of the Wild, how many Korok Seeds are there total? Winner gives out 3 sips.
First to shout it out: What do you get for collecting all 900 Korok Seeds in Breath of the Wild? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hestu's Gift — a golden poop. Literally.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is Link's full name? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Just 'Link' — he has no last name. Miyamoto confirmed this.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the original Legend of Zelda (NES), what happens if you name your save file 'ZELDA'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It starts you in the Second Quest (harder version of the game) immediately

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The music that plays in Zelda's Lullaby has how many notes in its main motif? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 6 notes

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What recurring Zelda enemy was inspired by the developer's childhood fear? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Wallmaster (disembodied hand) — Miyamoto was scared of a hand reaching out from the dark as a kid

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The PlayStation was originally designed as a CD add-on for what console? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Super Nintendo (SNES) — Sony and Nintendo's failed partnership led to the PlayStation

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the startup sound of the original PS1 designed to feel like? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A cathedral/church — the reverb and choir were intentionally spiritual-feeling

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the PS2's hidden easteregg on the dashboard menu that changed based on your save data? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The towers on the PS2 menu screen — each tower represents a save file. More saves = taller skyline

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What disc color did PS1 games that had anti-piracy protection use? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Black discs (most PS1 games had black-bottomed CDs as part of Sony's copy protection)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many PS2 consoles were sold worldwide, making it the best-selling console ever? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 155 million units

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the PS3's original launch price for the 60GB model in the US? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: $599 — leading to the famous 'five hundred and ninety-nine US dollars' meme

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the original Xbox's codename during development? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: DirectX Box — which got shortened to Xbox

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What infamous hardware failure plagued the Xbox 360? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The Red Ring of Death (RRoD) — a general hardware failure indicated by 3 red lights

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Xbox 360's achievement system was the first of its kind. What was the max Gamerscore for a retail game? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 1000 Gamerscore (1000G)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why did Microsoft skip 'Xbox 2' and go straight to Xbox 360? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: They didn't want consumers thinking 'Xbox 2' was a generation behind the 'PlayStation 3'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Halo 3, what hidden message can you find on the last level by holding down the right thumbstick? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A secret message from a Bungie dev that says 'Happy Birthday Lauren!'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the actual name of the Halo theme's famous chanting? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The monks — the chanting was recorded in a bathroom at the Bungie office for natural reverb

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What were Halo's Elite enemies originally called in-game lore? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Sangheili

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Halo was originally announced at what Apple event before becoming an Xbox exclusive? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Macworld 1999 — Steve Jobs introduced it as a Mac game

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What hidden Easter egg can you find in GTA: San Andreas involving a bridge? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A sign on the Gant Bridge that reads 'There are no Easter Eggs up here. Go away.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the name of the controversial hidden mini-game in GTA: San Andreas? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Hot Coffee — a hidden sexual mini-game that caused the game to be re-rated to AO

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What city in GTA IV is based on New York City? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Liberty City

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How much money did GTA V make in its first 24 hours of sales? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over $800 million — it reached $1 billion in 3 days, faster than any entertainment product ever

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the origin of the 'YOU DIED' screen font in Dark Souls? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It uses Optimus Princeps font — a Roman-style serif typeface

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Dark Souls, what NPC gives you the famous 'Praise the Sun' gesture? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Solaire of Astora

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real location inspired the architecture of Anor Londo in Dark Souls? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Milan Cathedral (Duomo di Milano) in Italy

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Elden Ring's story was co-written by the creator of Dark Souls and what fantasy author? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: George R.R. Martin (Game of Thrones author)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Dark Souls, what is the Pendant starting gift that the director jokingly recommended? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It literally does nothing — Miyazaki trolled players by saying it was the best starting gift

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many voice actors voiced ALL of Oblivion's 1,500+ NPCs? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 12 — with some actors voicing dozens of characters each

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What item in Skyrim weighs nothing but can't be removed from your inventory? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Elder Scrolls (quest items) — the actual Elder Scroll weighs 20 but can't be dropped during quests

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Skyrim, what happens if you kill a chicken in Riverwood? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The entire town attacks you — chickens are considered owned property and witnesses

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Skyrim bug causes mammoths to randomly fall from the sky? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A physics engine bug where giants launch creatures into orbit — sometimes they land elsewhere on the map

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many times has Skyrim been re-released across different platforms? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: At least 10+ versions — original, Legendary, Special Edition, VR, Switch, Anniversary, etc.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Minecraft Creeper? It was actually created by accident. What went wrong? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Notch accidentally swapped the height and length values when coding a pig — the tall, broken pig model became the Creeper

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the name of Minecraft's other dimension besides the Nether and Overworld? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The End

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What music artist composed Minecraft's iconic ambient soundtrack? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: C418 (Daniel Rosenfeld)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the maximum height you can build in Minecraft (as of recent updates)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Y=320 (build limit was raised from 256 in the 1.18 Caves & Cliffs update)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the rarest ore in Minecraft? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Emerald ore — it only spawns in mountain biomes and is rarer than diamonds

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why does Pac-Man have a kill screen at level 256? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: An integer overflow bug — the game uses an 8-bit level counter that can only go to 255. Level 256 corrupts half the screen

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the first video game Easter egg ever hidden in a game? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: In Adventure (Atari 2600, 1980) — developer Warren Robinett hid a secret room with 'Created by Warren Robinett' because Atari didn't credit developers

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What arcade game was so popular in Japan in 1978 that it caused a coin shortage? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Space Invaders

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the original Donkey Kong arcade, what is Mario trying to rescue? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Pauline (not Princess Peach — Pauline came first)

prompts.text: {player1}, what is the Konami Code? Recite the full sequence or take 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up Up Down Down Left Right Left Right B A (Start)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who holds the world record for the highest verified Pac-Man score — a perfect game? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Billy Mitchell achieved the first perfect Pac-Man score (3,333,360) in 1999 — though his records have been controversial

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the first commercially sold video game? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Computer Space (1971) — NOT Pong. Pong came a year later in 1972

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the Atari 2600 game that is blamed for the 1983 video game crash? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial — millions of unsold copies were famously buried in a New Mexico landfill

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the original Space Invaders, why do the aliens speed up as you kill them? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It wasn't intentional — the hardware could render fewer enemies faster, so they sped up as an accidental difficulty curve

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who created Tetris and where? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Alexey Pajitnov, a Soviet computer scientist, in Moscow in 1984

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What Cold War drama surrounded the rights to sell Tetris? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Multiple companies fought over the rights — Nintendo eventually won the handheld rights by negotiating directly with the Soviet government through ELORG

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the name of the phenomenon where you see Tetris blocks when you close your eyes after playing? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Tetris Effect — a real psychological phenomenon now named after the game

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Portal was based on a student project. What was it called? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Narbacular Drop — Valve hired the entire student team from DigiPen Institute

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is Valve's employee handbook famous for? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It describes their flat hierarchy and famously says desks have wheels so employees can roll to whatever project interests them

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long has it been since Valve released Half-Life 2: Episode 2 without making Episode 3? When was it? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 2007 — over 18 years and counting (Half-Life: Alyx came out in 2020 but wasn't Episode 3)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Super Smash Bros. was originally prototyped with generic characters. What was it called? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Dragon King: The Fighting Game — Sakurai swapped in Nintendo characters to make it more appealing

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Who was the last DLC character added to Super Smash Bros. Ultimate? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Sora from Kingdom Hearts

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many playable fighters are in Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (including DLC)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 89 fighters

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the original Animal Crossing (GameCube), what NES games could you find hidden in the game? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Full playable versions of NES classics like Donkey Kong, Balloon Fight, Excitebike, and more were hidden as furniture items

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real creature inspired the scorpion's terrifying chase behavior in Animal Crossing? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It mimics the actual aggression of real scorpions and tarantulas — the game exaggerates their chase behavior for comedic horror

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Animal Crossing: New Horizons, what is the maximum number of villagers on your island? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 10 villagers

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why is Sonic blue? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: To match Sega's cobalt blue logo — Sonic was designed to be Sega's mascot

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is Sonic's full name? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Sonic the Hedgehog — but in the Archie comics his name was 'Olgilvie Maurice Hedgehog' (this was retconned)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Tails' full name is Miles Prower. What is that a pun on? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 'Miles per hour' — a speed pun

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What platformer was the Sega Genesis/Mega Drive ORIGINALLY going to launch with instead of Sonic? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Alex Kidd — he was Sega's original mascot before Sonic replaced him

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Metal Gear Solid, what happens if you plug your controller into Port 2 during the Psycho Mantis fight? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Psycho Mantis can't 'read your mind' anymore — it stops him from predicting your moves. It's the intended solution.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Psycho Mantis in MGS reads your memory card. What does he comment on? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: He mentions specific games you've played — like Castlevania or Suikoden save data on your memory card

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What codec frequency do you call to save your game in Metal Gear Solid? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 140.96 (Mei Ling's frequency)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why is it called 'Final Fantasy'? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Square was going bankrupt and it was supposed to be their final game. It saved the company instead.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Final Fantasy VII, Aerith's death was one of the most shocking moments in gaming. How far into the game does it happen? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: End of Disc 1 (out of 3 discs)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a Chocobo? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A large rideable bird — a recurring creature across the entire Final Fantasy series

prompts.text: {player1}, the victory fanfare music in Final Fantasy has been in every mainline game. Can you hum it? Group judges. Fail = 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The classic 'da-da da da-da-da da da daaaa' jingle — composed by Nobuo Uematsu

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real historical era is Red Dead Redemption 2 set in? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 1899 — the dying days of the American Wild West

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In RDR2, your horse can permanently die. What happens to its bonding level and items? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Everything is lost — bonding level resets and any items stored on the horse are gone forever

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is Arthur Morgan's terminal illness in RDR2? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Tuberculosis (TB)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Resident Evil 4, what line does the merchant say when you approach him? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: 'What are ya buyin'?' and 'What are ya sellin'?'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The original Resident Evil on PS1 is famous for what hilariously bad feature? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The live-action cutscenes with terrible acting — 'Jill, here's a lockpick. It might be handy if you, the master of unlocking, take it with you.'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Fortnite was in development for how many years before Battle Royale was added? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 6 years — it started as 'Save the World' (PvE) in 2011. Battle Royale was added in 2017 after PUBG's success

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game inspired the entire Battle Royale genre? What movie is THAT game named after? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: PUBG (PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds), inspired by the 2000 Japanese film 'Battle Royale'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real-world fungus is the Cordyceps infection in The Last of Us based on? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Ophiocordyceps unilateralis — a real parasitic fungus that controls ant behavior (zombie ants)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In The Last of Us Part II, how long did Naughty Dog spend developing it? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 6 years (2014-2020) — it was Naughty Dog's longest development cycle at the time

prompts.text: First to shout it out: God of War (2018) was filmed in one continuous camera shot with no cuts. True or false? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: True — the entire game from start to finish has zero camera cuts, including cutscenes

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the original God of War trilogy, Kratos killed most of the Greek pantheon. Name 3 gods he killed? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Ares, Poseidon, Hades, Helios, Hermes, Hercules, Hephaestus, Zeus, and more

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the first Call of Duty game to feature the Zombies mode? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Call of Duty: World at War (2008) — it was originally a hidden bonus mode unlocked after beating the campaign

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The 'No Russian' mission in Modern Warfare 2 was so controversial that some versions let you skip it. What country actually banned the level? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Russia — the Russian version removed the entire mission. Japan also altered it.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In BioShock, what is the philosophical inspiration behind the city of Rapture? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Ayn Rand's Objectivism — Andrew Ryan is an anagram of 'Ayn Rand' and Rapture embodies her philosophy

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the twist reveal involving 'Would you kindly' in BioShock? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The phrase is a mind-control trigger — every time Atlas said 'Would you kindly,' the player was being controlled. It's a meta-commentary on player obedience in games.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Undertale was made almost entirely by one person. Who? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Toby Fox — he composed the music, wrote the story, and programmed the game

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Undertale, what happens to the game permanently if you complete a Genocide run? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Even after resetting, the game remembers — the True Pacifist ending is permanently altered. Chara appears and the ending photo is different.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Stardew Valley was made by one person over how many years? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 4.5 years — Eric 'ConcernedApe' Barone did all art, music, programming, and design solo

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game franchise inspired Stardew Valley? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Harvest Moon (now Story of Seasons) — Barone made it because he felt the series had declined

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Overwatch was built from the ashes of what cancelled Blizzard MMO? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Project Titan — after 7 years of development, Titan was scrapped and a small team salvaged it into Overwatch

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is CD Projekt Red's country of origin? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Poland — they're based in Warsaw

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Witcher games are based on a book series by what author? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Andrzej Sapkowski — a Polish fantasy author who wrote the original novels

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Cyberpunk 2077 had one of gaming's roughest launches. What console version was so buggy it was pulled from the PlayStation Store? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: PS4 — Sony actually removed it from the PS Store and offered refunds, unprecedented for a AAA game

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The Animus in Assassin's Creed lets you relive ancestral memories through what? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Genetic memory stored in DNA

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The first Assassin's Creed was originally planned as a sequel to what other Ubisoft franchise? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Prince of Persia — it was originally 'Prince of Persia: Assassin' before becoming its own IP

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In the original Mortal Kombat, the 'blood' was actually what color on the SNES version? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Gray/white — Nintendo censored the blood to look like sweat. The Genesis version kept the red blood with a cheat code (ABACABB)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real martial artist was digitized (photographed) to create the character Johnny Cage in Mortal Kombat? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Daniel Pesina — all original MK characters were real actors digitized into the game

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What input motion do you use for a Hadouken in Street Fighter? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Quarter-circle forward + punch (down, down-forward, forward + punch)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The 'Evo Moment 37' is considered the greatest moment in fighting game history. What game was it in? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Street Fighter III: 3rd Strike — Daigo parried all 15 hits of Justin Wong's Chun-Li super art with 1 pixel of health

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Id Software's DOOM (1993) popularized the FPS genre. What did people call FPS games before they had their own genre name? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 'Doom clones' — every FPS was called a Doom clone until the genre got its own name

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The original DOOM was spread primarily through what distribution method? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Shareware — the first episode was free, distributed via floppy disks and early internet. You paid for the full game.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Mass Effect, what species' name sounds like 'tourism' and they're the galaxy's merchant race? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Volus (but the merchant-focused species are actually the Volus). The Turians are the military race.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the massive controversy about Mass Effect 3's ending? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The three endings were nearly identical with just different colors (red, blue, green) — fans were so outraged that BioWare released an Extended Cut DLC to add more closure

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What does 'Sega' actually stand for? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 'Service Games' — the company started as an American coin-op firm in Japan serving US military bases

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the Sega Dreamcast's most revolutionary feature for its time? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Built-in modem for online play — it was the first console with internet connectivity out of the box (1999)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What killed the Sega Dreamcast despite it being technologically ahead of its time? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The PS2's hype, Sega's damaged reputation from the Saturn, and easy piracy (games could be burned to regular CDs)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Gordon Freeman's iconic weapon from Half-Life is what? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The crowbar

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Half-Life's intro is famous for what revolutionary approach to storytelling? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The tram ride — no cutscenes, the entire story unfolds in first-person with no camera taken away from the player

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Among Us launched in 2018 but didn't go viral until when? What caused the surge? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 2020 — Twitch streamers and content creators during COVID lockdowns made it blow up, 2+ years after launch

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Wii Sports is the best-selling single-platform game of all time (bundled). How many copies? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 82 million copies (bundled with the Wii console)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Wii Sports bowling, what happens if you throw the ball backwards? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Miis in the audience gasp and react in shock/fear

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game was made in 48 hours for a game jam and later expanded into a full release about going up a mountain? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Getting Over It with Bennett Foddy — inspired by Sexy Hiking, a 2002 B-game

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Hollow Knight's map is called what? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Hallownest — an underground kingdom of bugs

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game's soundtrack was so popular it charted on Billboard? The game features a skeleton named Sans? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Undertale — 'Megalovania' became a cultural phenomenon

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In Elden Ring, what does 'Try finger, but hole' mean? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: It's the most famous player-left message — a recurring troll message using the game's preset word system. It means exactly what you think.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game secretly deletes your save file and pretends to uninstall itself as part of its story? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Doki Doki Literature Club — the game messes with its own files as part of its psychological horror

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What indie game about climbing a mountain was praised for its hidden assist mode that doesn't judge you? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Celeste — the assist mode can slow the game, add dashes, or grant invincibility with no penalty

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Hades was the first video game to win what literary award? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Hugo Award for Best Video Game (2021) — a prestigious science fiction and fantasy award

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game requires you to look at the PHYSICAL game case or manual to solve a puzzle? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Metal Gear Solid — Meryl's codec frequency (140.15) is printed on the back of the actual game case

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game has a character who remembers your save/load behavior and calls you out for it? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Undertale — Flowey remembers if you've reset the game and comments on your save manipulation

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What year did Minecraft officially release (1.0 version, not beta)? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: November 18, 2011

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What did the developers of Goldeneye 007 originally plan to add at the end of every mission? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A screen where the player would shake hands with all the enemies they killed — it was scrapped for being too weird

prompts.text: First to shout it out: The PS5's DualSense controller has haptic feedback. What game was specifically designed to showcase it at launch? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Astro's Playroom — it came pre-installed on every PS5

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the best-selling video game of all time (not including bundled games)? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Minecraft — over 300 million copies sold

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game has an NPC named 'Error' that says 'I am Error' — and it's NOT a glitch? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Zelda II: The Adventure of Link (NES) — 'I am Error' is his actual name, a translation quirk

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In what game can you pet the dog, and the internet made it a requirement for all games? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Many games! But the @CanYouPetTheDog Twitter account started in 2019 tracking this — one early notable one was Fable II (2008)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What infamous game delay was joked about for over a decade before the game actually released in 2020? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Cyberpunk 2077 — announced in 2012, teased in 2013, released 7 years later after multiple delays

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game's composer hid a picture of his face in the soundtrack's spectrogram? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: DOOM (2016) — Mick Gordon hid pentagrams and other images in the music's spectrogram analysis

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the Game Boy's battery life compared to its competitor the Sega Game Gear? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Game Boy: ~30 hours on 4 AA batteries. Game Gear: ~3-5 hours on 6 AA batteries. This is a big reason the Game Boy won.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What number is written on Vault Boy's jumpsuit in Fallout? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: No number on Vault Boy himself — he represents Vault-Tec. But the player in Fallout 3 starts in Vault 101.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: In The Sims, what language do the characters speak? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Simlish — a gibberish language that voice actors actually perform. Some real musicians recorded songs in Simlish.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What real company tried to trademark the word 'Scrolls' and sued Mojang? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Bethesda/ZeniMax — they sued Mojang because 'Scrolls' might confuse people with 'The Elder Scrolls.' Mojang settled.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game features a secret developer room accessible only through an extremely long sequence of inputs? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Many games have dev rooms — one famous one is Batman: Arkham Asylum's hidden room with blueprints for Arkham City that wasn't found for years

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the maximum level in the original World of Warcraft (2004, no expansions)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Level 60

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Leeroy Jenkins is one of gaming's most famous memes. What game is it from? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: World of Warcraft — the video shows him ruining a carefully planned raid by charging in screaming his own name

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What game's loading screen tips are intentionally unhelpful and sarcastic? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Many games do this now, but the Souls series popularized cryptic/unhelpful loading tips. 'You Died' is a loading screen.

prompts.text: {player1}, do your best video game character impression or take 3 sips.
{player1}, take {sip_count} sips. No power-ups can save you.
{player1}, what's the last game you rage quit? No answer? Take 3 sips.
{player1}, what's your most played game ever? Everyone judges — if it's basic, take 2 sips.
{player1}, describe a game without saying its name. If nobody guesses in 15 seconds, take 3 sips.
{player1}, what game are you embarrassed to admit you love? Take 1 sip for the confession.
{player1}, make a video game sound effect. If nobody recognizes it, take 3 sips.
{player1}, what game has the best soundtrack? If someone disagrees, you both drink 2 sips.
{player1}, name the last boss you struggled with. Take 1 sip per attempt it took you (max 5).
{player1}, what game would you want to live in? If someone says 'that would suck,' take 2 sips.
{player1}, what's the worst game you've ever paid full price for? Take 2 sips for the trauma.
{player1}, say a gaming hot take. If the group disagrees, take 3 sips.
{player1}, if you could only play one game for a year, what is it? Group votes — bad pick = 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever cried at a video game? Yes = take 2 proud sips. No = take 2 soulless sips.
{player1}, name a game you've 100% completed. Can't? Take 3 sips, you casual.
{player1}, what's the longest gaming session you've had? Over 8 hours? Take 3 sips for your health.
{player1}, describe your gaming setup in 3 words. Group rates it 1-10. Under 5 = take 3 sips.
{player1}, what's your favorite multiplayer memory? Tell it or take 3 sips.
{player1}, have you ever been so scared by a game you turned it off? Yes = 2 sips. No = liar, 3 sips.
{player1}, what game do you think is overrated? If anyone gasps, take 3 sips.
{player1}, name a game you pre-ordered and regretted. Can't? Take 2 sips.
{player1}, what was the first game you ever played? If it's older than everyone else's, give out 3 sips.
{player1}, do you skip cutscenes? Yes = 3 sips from the story lovers. No = 1 sip of respect.
{player1}, have you ever broken a controller? Yes = take 3 sips of shame.
{player1}, what's your highest kill game in any shooter? Lowest at the table takes 3 sips.
{player1}, how many unfinished games are in your backlog? Take 1 sip per 10 (max 5).
{player1}, say the most iconic video game line you can think of. If someone else says it first, take 2 sips.
{player1}, what game villain do you secretly agree with? Explain or take 3 sips.
{player1}, what game do you wish you could experience for the first time again? Take 1 nostalgic sip.
{player1}, have you ever looked up a walkthrough and pretended you figured it out yourself? Yes = 3 sips of fraud.
{player1}, you got a kill streak! Give out 5 sips however you want.
{player1}, you found a loot crate! Give out 3 sips to whoever you want.

{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever skips cutscenes.
{player1}, give 2 sips to whoever takes the longest in character creation.
{player1}, give 3 sips to whoever would be the worst teammate in a raid.
{player1}, give out {sip_count} sips — you're the party leader.

{player1}, give out as many sips as Victory Royales you have in Fortnite. Max 6. Zero? You take 3.

{player1} is the Party Leader. They decide turn order for {rule_turns} rounds.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} has been kicked from the party.

prompts.text: No saying any game names for {rule_turns} rounds. Slip up = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Dungeon Master. They add 1 sip to anyone's penalty for {rule_turns} rounds.
Everyone must say 'GG' after taking sips for {rule_turns} rounds. Forget = extra sip.
No using your phone for {rule_turns} rounds. Touch it = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Healer. They can halve one person's sips once per round for {rule_turns} rounds.
Before every sip, you must say a game character name. Repeat = 2 sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
{player1} is the Final Boss. All sips directed at them are halved, but they can't give out sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
Everyone must raise their drink and say 'Power up!' before sipping. {rule_turns} rounds.
No laughing for {rule_turns} rounds. Laugh = 2 sips.
{player1} has a shield. They can block one sip assignment per round for {rule_turns} rounds.
Must whisper for {rule_turns} rounds. Normal voice = 2 sips. Stealth mode.
No first names — use gamer tags or character names only. {rule_turns} rounds. Slip = 2 sips.
{player1} picks a banned word. Anyone who says it for {rule_turns} rounds takes 2 sips.
Double XP! All sip counts are doubled for {rule_turns} rounds.
{player1} is the Tank. They absorb 1 sip from anyone who's assigned 3+ sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
Must make a game sound effect before every sip. {rule_turns} rounds.
{player1} is the Speedrunner. They can call 'skip!' once per round to skip a prompt. {rule_turns} rounds.
Everyone must say 'critical hit!' when giving out sips. Forget = 1 sip. {rule_turns} rounds.
{player1} is the NPC. They must give a quest to each person (a dare). Refuse = 3 sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
Friendly fire! You can redirect your sips to the person next to you once per round. {rule_turns} rounds.
No saying 'drink' or 'sip' for {rule_turns} rounds. Say it = 2 sips.
{player1} is the Inventory Master. They can 'store' 2 sips and assign them to anyone later. {rule_turns} rounds.
Must do a victory pose after giving out sips. {rule_turns} rounds.
Half-time! All penalties are halved for {rule_turns} rounds. Enjoy the break.

{player1}, the boss beat you. Take 5 sips.

Boss fight! {player1} vs the group. Name 10 games. Group names 10 games. Whoever finishes first wins. Losers take 3 sips.
Heaven! Last person to point up drinks 3 sips. It's a quick-time event!
Floor is lava! Last person to lift their feet drinks 3 sips.
{player1}, Would You Rather: only play one game for life OR never game again? Group decides your punishment — 3 or 5 sips.
Speed round! Everyone name a game. Go around twice. Any repeat = 3 sips for the repeater.
{player1}, hot take time: name the most overrated game ever. If someone at the table loves it, you both drink 3.
Respawn round! Everyone who has sipped in the last 3 rounds — you're healed. Give out 2 sips.
Random encounter! {player1} picks a number 1-5. That's how many sips they take. Bad RNG.
Save point! Everyone take a breather. But first, take 1 sip to save your progress.
Final boss music! The next 3 prompts have doubled sips. Brace yourselves.
